title: Glossary - Nox Protocol
description: Definitions of technical terms used in the Nox protocol

terms.definition: On-chain access control mechanism allowing to define who can see (Viewer) or use (Admin) a handle. Permissions can be persistent or transient.
ACL role with full control over a handle: can perform compute, grant permissions (allow), and add viewers.
Second category of computation primitives: more complex operations like transfer, mint, burn for confidential tokens.
cToken business model: iExec (1st B) provides technology to Builders/Protocols (2nd B) who serve their end-users (3rd B).
Confidential DeFi - Decentralized finance with transaction and balance privacy. Nox is iExec's cDeFi protocol.
Unique identifier of the blockchain chain (4 bytes) included in a handle's structure (bytes 26-29) to guarantee cross-chain uniqueness.
Encrypted form of confidential data (balance, amount). Stored off-chain in the Handle Gateway database, never publicly exposed on the blockchain.
One of Nox's pillars: ability to integrate with the existing DeFi ecosystem and third-party protocols.
Confidential computation operation on handles (add, sub, div, transfer, etc.) executed by a Runner in a TEE.
Wrapped version of an ERC-20 with encrypted balances and amounts.
Practice of copying investment strategies of other traders. DeFi transparency facilitates this practice, which privacy limits.
Basic protocol operations: add(), sub(), div(), wrapAsPublicHandle(), select(). First category of computation primitives.
Third category of primitives: custom functions developed by protocol users.
Component of the EIP-712 digest including name, version, chainId and contract address. Used to sign Handle Gateway messages.
Framework for running Docker containers inside TEEs. Used by Nox to deploy off-chain components (Handle Gateway, Runners, KMS) in Intel TDX CVMs (Confidential Virtual Machines).
Elliptic Curve Integrated Encryption Scheme - Encryption framework used by KMS that composes different algorithms such as AES-GCM-256.
Ethereum standard for typed signatures. Used to verify Handle Gateway signatures with digest including handle, owner, app, and createdAt.
Ethereum standard for transient storage (tstore). Used for transient permissions that are erased at the end of the transaction.
Hardware-isolated environment in a TEE where secure components run (Handle Gateway, Runners, KMS).
Fungible token standard on Ethereum. cToken allows wrapping any ERC-20 into a confidential version.
Practice where validators/miners anticipate and execute transactions before users to profit from them. Privacy protects against this.
General Data Protection Regulation - European regulation on personal data protection. Nox enables compliance via programmable auditability.
Unique 32-byte identifier stored on-chain that points to an off-chain ciphertext. Structure: truncated hash (26 bytes) + Chain ID (4) + Type (1) + Version (1).
Secure entry point of the system operating in a TEE. Encrypts user data, stores ciphertexts, and serves as interface to the Handle↔Ciphertext database.
Dedicated service that monitors the blockchain to detect primitive events (Add, Sub, Transfer...) and sends them to the message queue.
Trust Domain Extensions - Intel's TEE technology used by Nox to create hardware-isolated secure enclaves.
One of Nox's pillars: compatibility with existing blockchains, notably Arbitrum.
Cryptographic hash function used to calculate preHandle and EIP-712 digests in the Nox protocol.
Vault that manages decryption delegation.
Value extracted by validators/miners by reordering transactions. cToken privacy protects against MEV.
Markets in Crypto-Assets - European regulation on crypto-assets. Nox enables compliance via selective disclosure.
Cryptographic technology allowing multiple parties to jointly compute without revealing their inputs.
Main on-chain smart contract for receiving and coordinating confidential computation requests.
Storage or computations performed off the blockchain. Ciphertexts are stored off-chain in the Handle Gateway database.
Handle ownership determined by the associated public key. Can be transferred via the sponsoring mechanism.
Data submitted in a transaction containing necessary information (public key, handles, etc.).
ACL permissions stored via sstore (EVM storage) that survive the end of the transaction.
Unencrypted data in clear. In Nox, data exists in plaintext only in the Runner's TEE enclave during execution.
Hash calculated via keccak256(operator, operands, NoxCompte, msg.sender, block.timestamp, outputIndex) before being truncated to form the final handle.
Basic computation operations: addition, subtraction, division, conditional selection.
Wrapped version of an ERC-20 with encrypted balances and amounts.
Initiating an HTTPS connection between the SDK and the Handle Gateway after verifying the trustworthiness of Nox components via Remote Attestation.
Cryptographically verifiable proof that a component runs in a genuine TEE hardware with the expected code.
Executor that performs confidential computations on encrypted data in a secure TEE environment.
Real-world assets tokenized on blockchain (real estate, bonds, etc.). Key segment targeted by cToken requiring privacy.
Software Development Kit - Client-side software development kit allowing developers to integrate cToken into their applications.
Fragments of the private key distributed among KMS nodes in the threshold protocol. No single node possesses the complete key.
Ability to reveal information only to authorized parties (auditors, regulators) via the ACL system, without public exposure.
Difference between expected price and actual execution price of a transaction. Privacy protects against slippage caused by front-running.
EVM opcode for persistent storage. Used to store persistent ACL permissions that survive the transaction.
Secure execution environment with hardware isolation (Intel TDX). Guarantees privacy and integrity of sensitive operations.
Threshold cryptography where the private key is fragmented among n nodes. At least t nodes must collaborate for any operation.
Traditional Finance - Traditional finance (banks, funds). Major target of cToken which requires privacy to enter DeFi.
ACL permissions using tstore (EIP-1153), less expensive in gas, automatically erased at the end of the transaction.
EVM opcode for transient storage (EIP-1153). Used for transient ACL permissions less expensive in gas.
Total value locked in a DeFi protocol. Key metric to measure adoption. ETH = $69B, ARB = $2.9B.
Read-only ACL role: can request handle decryption but cannot perform compute or modify permissions.
Conversion operations between public ERC-20 tokens and their confidential versions. Reversible at any time.
Primitive that creates a handle for a public plaintext value, useful for initializing counters to zero.
Zero-Knowledge Proofs - Technology allowing to prove a statement without revealing the underlying information.
